audit_items.proof_trace: `ber_alloc_t(LBER_USE_DER)` can return NULL on allocation failure (io.c:271-273), but both `ldap_cancel` (L48-49) and `ldap_cancel_s` (L68-69) pass the result directly to `ber_printf()` without a NULL check; `ber_printf` asserts `ber != NULL` (encode.c:511) which is compiled out in release builds, leaving a NULL dereference. Comparable functions in the same library (`passwd.c:99-102`, `dds.c:104-107`) correctly guard against this with an explicit NULL check and `LDAP_NO_MEMORY` return.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker or system condition causing memory exhaustion on an LDAP client or proxy host triggers OOM in `ber_alloc_t`, causing `ldap_cancel`/`ldap_cancel_s` to pass NULL to `ber_printf`, resulting in a NULL pointer dereference and process crash (DoS).
